\chapter{Boolean Analysis — Circuit Layer Reduction}
%%% generated by the blueprint pipeline from TCSlib.BooleanAnalysis.LMN.CircuitLayerReduction
\section{Overview}

This module proves the core iterative reduction theorem of the LMN argument: under a
composed Bernoulli random restriction, a function computed by a bounded-depth, bounded-fan-in
circuit has small decision-tree depth with high probability. The proof proceeds by strong
induction on the circuit depth $d$, with a base case ($d=2$) handled by the switching lemma
and an inductive step ($d\ge 3$) that factors the restriction parameter and applies a
two-stage argument to the children of the root gate.

\section{Declarations}

\begin{definition}[Composed restriction parameter]
\lean{LMN.composedDelta}
\leanok
For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Positivity of the composed parameter]
\lean{LMN.composedDelta_pos}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.composedDelta}
For $w>0$ and $l>0$, the composed restriction parameter $\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)$ is
positive.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[The composed parameter is at most one]
\lean{LMN.composedDelta_le_one}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.composedDelta}
For $w\ge 1$, $l\ge 1$, and $d\ge 2$, the composed restriction parameter satisfies
$\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)\le 1$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Recursive step factorization (left)]
\lean{LMN.composedDelta_step}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.composedDelta}
For natural numbers $w,l$ and depth $d\ge 3$,
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot \mathrm{composedDelta}(l,l,d-1).
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Recursive step factorization (right)]
\lean{LMN.composedDelta_step_right}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.composedDelta}
For $d\ge 3$ and $l>0$, the composed parameter factors as the $(d-1)$ case times $1/(40l)$:
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d-1)\cdot\frac{1}{40l}.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{definition}[Layer-2 data]
\lean{LMN.Layer2Data}
\leanok
\uses{DNF, DNF.width}
A bundle describing the second layer of a circuit over $n$ variables: a number of gates,
a family of DNF formulas indexed by the gates, a common width bound $\mathrm{width}>0$ such
that every gate's width is at most $\mathrm{width}$, together with the hypothesis that within
each term of each gate distinct literals have distinct variables.
\end{definition}

\begin{theorem}[One-step switching for layer-2 data]
\lean{LMN.normalform_one_step_switching}
\leanok
\uses{DNF.eval, LMN.Layer2Data, LMN.switching_bernoulli_union_bound, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
For layer-2 data and a Bernoulli parameter $p$ with $0<p\le 1/(40\cdot\mathrm{width})$ and
$p\le 1$, the probability that some gate's restricted function has decision-tree depth greater
than $l$ is at most
\[
  \mathrm{numGates}\cdot\left((1/2)^{l} + \exp(-np/3)\right).
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[One-step CNF replaceability for layer-2 data]
\lean{LMN.normalform_one_step_cnf_replaceability}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF.eval, LMN.Layer2Data, LMN.one_step_reduction_failure_bound, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn}
For layer-2 data and a Bernoulli parameter $p$ with $0<p\le 1/(40\cdot\mathrm{width})$ and
$p\le 1$, the probability that it fails that every gate's restricted function is computed by
some width-$l$ CNF is at most
\[
  \mathrm{numGates}\cdot\left((1/2)^{l} + \exp(-np/3)\right).
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Aggregating per-layer bounds]
\lean{LMN.full_iterative_bound}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.multi_stage_failure_bound}
Given layer sizes summing to at most $s$, if each per-layer quantity is bounded by
$\mathrm{layerSize}(i)\cdot\alpha$ (with $\alpha\ge 0$) and a final term is bounded by $\beta$,
then the total $\sum_i \mathrm{per\_layer}(i) + \mathrm{final}$ is at most $s\cdot\alpha+\beta$.
\end{theorem}

\begin{lemma}[Restriction commutes with restriction composition]
\lean{LMN.restrictFn_composeRestr'}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.composeRestr, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction.extend, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn}
For a Boolean function $f$ and restrictions $\rho_1,\rho_2$, restricting by the composed
restriction equals restricting by $\rho_1$ and then by $\rho_2$:
\[
  \mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,(\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2)
    = \mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho_1)\,\rho_2.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}[Two-stage probability bound]
\lean{LMN.two_stage_bound'}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.composeRestr, LMN.restriction_compose_eq, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb_le_one', SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrWeight, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrWeight_nonneg', SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrWeight_sum_one}
Let $p_1,p_2\in(0,1]$, let $E$ and $A$ be predicates on restrictions, and let $\beta\ge 0$.
If for every $\rho_1$ with $\neg A(\rho_1)$ the conditional probability
$\Pr_{p_2}[E(\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2)]\le\beta$, then
\[
  \Pr_{p_1 p_2}[E] \le \Pr_{p_1}[A] + \beta.
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{lemma}[Monotonicity in the depth threshold]
\lean{LMN.bernoulliRestrProb_dtDepth_mono}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.bernoulliRestrProb_mono, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
For $0\le p\le 1$, a Boolean function $f$, and thresholds $l_1\le l_2$, the probability that
the restricted function exceeds depth $l_2$ is at most the probability that it exceeds depth
$l_1$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Congruence under pointwise-equal functions]
\lean{LMN.bernoulliRestrProb_congr_fn'}
\leanok
\uses{SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
If $f$ and $g$ agree on every input, then the probability that the restriction of $f$ exceeds
depth $t$ equals the corresponding probability for $g$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Switching bound for depth-2 circuits]
\lean{LMN.depth2_circuit_switching_bound}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, BoolCircuit.Circuit.maxFanin, BoolCircuit.Circuit.size, BoolCircuit.Lit.eval, BoolCircuit.Lit.toLiteral, CNF.eval, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.bernoulliRestrProb_congr_fn', LMN.depth2AndToCNF, LMN.depth2AndToCNF_eval, LMN.depth2AndToCNF_width_le, LMN.depth2OrToDNF, LMN.depth2OrToDNF_eval, LMN.depth2OrToDNF_width_le, LMN.switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_cnf_general, LMN.switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_dnf_general, Literal.eval, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, Term.eval, Term.width, dtDepth}
If $f$ is computed by a circuit of depth at most $2$, size at most $s$, and fan-in at most $w$,
then for a Bernoulli parameter $p$ with $0<p\le 1/(40w)$ and $p\le 1$,
\[
  \Pr_{p}\!\left[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\right]
    \le (1/2)^{t} + \exp(-np/3).
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Inductive base case: depth-2 bound]
\lean{LMN.circuit_reduction_ind_base}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, BoolCircuit.Circuit.maxFanin, BoolCircuit.Circuit.size, DecisionTree.eval, LMN.composedDelta, LMN.composedDelta_le_one, LMN.depth2_circuit_switching_bound, LMN.dtDepth_restrictFn_le', SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrWeight, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrWeight_sum_one, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
The base case ($d=2$) of the inductive bound: if $f$ is computed by a depth-$2$ circuit of
size at most $s$ and fan-in at most $w$ (with $s,w,l>0$ and $n>0$), then under the composed
restriction parameter $\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,2)$,
\[
  \Pr\!\left[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\right]
  \le (s-1)(1/2)^{l} + (1/2)^{t} + (s-1)\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120w}\right)
    + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120l}\right).
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Union bound over a list of circuits]
\lean{LMN.bernoulliRestrProb_list_union_bound}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, LMN.bernoulliRestrProb_union_bound_fin, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb}
For $0\le p\le 1$, a list of circuits $\mathit{cs}$, and a predicate $\mathrm{bad}$ on circuits
and restrictions, the probability that some circuit in $\mathit{cs}$ is bad is bounded by the
folded sum of the per-circuit probabilities $\sum_{c\in \mathit{cs}}\Pr_{p}[\mathrm{bad}\,c]$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Inductive step: depth $d\ge 3$]
\lean{LMN.circuit_reduction_ind_step}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, BoolCircuit.Circuit.maxFanin, BoolCircuit.Circuit.size, DecisionTree.depth, DecisionTree.eval, LMN.bernoulliRestrProb_list_union_bound, LMN.child_size_le_parent, LMN.children_depth_le, LMN.children_maxFanin_le, LMN.children_size_sum_le, LMN.composedDelta, LMN.composedDelta_le_one, LMN.composedDelta_pos, LMN.composedDelta_step_right, LMN.compress_and_switch, LMN.dtDepth_restrictFn_le', LMN.two_stage_bound', SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb_le_one', SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
The inductive step: for depth $d\ge 3$, assuming the bound holds for depth $d-1$ (the
hypothesis $\mathit{ih}$ applied to each child of the root), a circuit of depth at most $d$,
size at most $s$, and fan-in at most $w$ satisfies, under $\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)$,
\[
  \Pr\!\left[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\right]
  \le (s-1)(1/2)^{l} + (1/2)^{t} + (s-1)\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120w}\right)
    + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120l}\right).
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}[Iterative reduction bound (tight form)]
\lean{LMN.circuit_reduction_ind}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, BoolCircuit.Circuit.maxFanin, BoolCircuit.Circuit.size, LMN.circuit_reduction_ind_base, LMN.circuit_reduction_ind_step, LMN.composedDelta, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
By strong induction on the depth $d\ge 2$: if $f$ is computed by a circuit of depth at most
$d$, size at most $s$, and fan-in at most $w$ (with $s,w,l,n>0$), then under
$\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)$,
\[
  \Pr\!\left[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\right]
  \le (s-1)(1/2)^{l} + (1/2)^{t} + (s-1)\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120w}\right)
    + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120l}\right).
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Iterative reduction bound (relaxed form)]
\lean{LMN.circuit_reduction_aux}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, BoolCircuit.Circuit.maxFanin, BoolCircuit.Circuit.size, LMN.circuit_reduction_ind, LMN.composedDelta, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
Replacing the coefficient $s-1$ by $s$ in the previous bound: under the same hypotheses and
the parameter $\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)$,
\[
  \Pr\!\left[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\right]
  \le s(1/2)^{l} + (1/2)^{t} + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120w}\right)
    + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120l}\right).
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Circuit reduction core bound]
\lean{LMN.circuit_reduction_core}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, BoolCircuit.Circuit.maxFanin, BoolCircuit.Circuit.size, LMN.circuit_reduction_aux, LMN.composedDelta, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
The user-facing form of the iterative reduction: for $f$ computed by a circuit of depth at
most $d\ge 2$, size at most $s$, and fan-in at most $w$, under $\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)$,
\[
  \Pr\!\left[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\right]
  \le s(1/2)^{l} + (1/2)^{t} + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120w}\right)
    + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120l}\right).
\]
\end{theorem}
